registerdb:Block.registerdb:Name: MACW_MAC
registerdb:Block.registerdb:Short_Description: Network Interface Port: Configuration of MAC for 8 lanes, and LED interface.
registerdb:Block.registerdb:Full_Description: Network Interface Port: Configuration of MAC for 8 lanes, and LED interface.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_DATA
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Data: Data to write to the indirect register/SRAM or data read from the indirect data register/SRAM.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description:
Indirect Data: Data to write to the indirect register/SRAM or data read from the indirect data register/SRAM
IND_DATA_0 - First part of data. ... Summary: The indirect register mechanism is used for accessing SRAMs and indirect registers. Indirect registers are 64-bits wide, internally divided into multiple fields. However, they do not have an address directly mapped to the host configuration memory space. Instead, they are accessed indirectly through the indirect access interface registers. SRAMs are memory structures consisting of several addressable SRAM lines. Each SRAM line can be made up of several fields.
Read Sequence Indirect Registers: 1. Specify the indirect register address to read - IND_ADDR. 2. Specify the indirect register command - IND_CMD. 3. Check the IND_CMD status - IND_STS. 4. Read the requested data - IND_DATA.
Write Sequence Indirect Registers: 1. Specify the data to write - IND_DATA. 2. Specify the indirect register address to write - IND_ADDR. 3. Specify the indirect register command - IND_CMD. 4. Check the IND_CMD status - IND_STS.
Read Sequence SRAMs: 1. Specify the SRAM line to read - IND_ADDR. 2. Specify the indirect SRAM command and SRAM ID - IND_CMD. 3. Check the IND_CMD status - IND_STS. 4. Read the requested data - IND_DATA.
Write Sequence SRAMs: 1. Specify the data to write - IND_DATA. 2. Specify the first SRAM line to write - IND_ADDR. 3. Specify the end SRAM line if relevant - IND_CMD_RANGE if field OP = 0x4/0x5 in register IND_CMD. 4. Specify the SRAM fields to modify if relevant - IND_CMD_BITMAP if field OP = 0x3/0x5 in register IND_CMD. 5. Specify the indirect register command and SRAM ID - IND_CMD. 6. Check the IND_CMD status - IND_STS.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Value: • SRAM Self-init: Bitmap of SRAMs to initialize (line (OP = 0x0 in register IND_CMD). E.g. if the b0 = 1 in the SRAM bitmap this indicates the first SRAM in the block. • SRAM: Data to write to SRAM or data read from the SRAM (OP = 0x1/2/3/4/5 in register IND_CMD). • Indirect Register: Data to write to the indirect register or data read from the indirect data register (OP = 0x8/0x9 in register IND_CMD).

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Indirect Command: Indirect register/SRAM command. The status is in register IND_STS. Indirect Address. Specifies the indirect register address or SRAM line/start-line

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Operation: 0x0 - SRAM self-init sets to RegDB Cfg Val (configuration value) for SRAMs specified in IND_DATA registers. 0x1 - SRAM read 0x2 - SRAM write 0x3 - SRAM modify for selected SRAM fields in register IND_CMD_BITMAP. 0x4 - SRAM write range. SRAM start-line in register IND_ADDR and end-line in register IND_CMD_RANGE. 0x5 - SRAM modify range for selected SRAM fields in register IND_CMD_BITMAP. SRAM start-line in register IND_ADDR and end-line in register IND_CMD_RANGE. 0x6 - 0x7 - RESERVED. 0x8 - Indirect register read. 0x9 - Indirect register write. 0xA - Indirect command as defined in RegDB Ind Cmds (EZchip internal use only). 0xB - 0xFF - RESERVED
The indirect register or SRAM's group as specified in RegDB.
The indirect register or SRAM's GroupInstances as specified in RegDB
The SRAM's Id as specified in RegDB

Value: • For SRAM read/write/modify single operations - line to access. • For SRAM write/modify range operations - SRAM start-line of operation. The SRAM end-line is defined in register IND_CMD_RANGE. • For Indirect registers - Address as specified in RegDB.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD_RANGE
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Indirect Command Range: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Memory End Address: The SRAM end-line address for SRAM write/modify range

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_CMD_BITMAP
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Command Bitmap: SRAM field bitmap for modify command.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: SRAM field bitmap for modify command (OP = 0x3/0x5 in register IND_CMD).

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description:
Value: SRAM field bitmap for modify command.
Bit Value: 0 - Disable modify for this SRAM field. 1 - Enable modify for this SRAM field.
Bit Description: b0 - First SRAM field in the line. b1 - Second SRAM field in the line.
...
Owner ID. 0: semaphore is free Others: semaphore is busy

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_STS
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Indirect Status: Indicates the status of the IND_CMD
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Indirect Status: Indicates the status of the IND_CMD

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Ready: Indicates that the command completed and a new command can be generated. 0 - Busy. 1 - Done. This bit is updated on every write (register IND_CMD) to the command register.
Indicates if the last command succeeded or failed. 0 - Error. 1 - Success. SUCCESS bit should be referred only when bit RDY = 1.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: IND_TIME_TH
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Host timeout configuration

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: BLOCK_ID
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Block Identity: Block and sub-block ID number for internal debugging purposes
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: nterface engine 0 (west) - Block ID 0x400 Interface engine 1 (west) - Block ID 0x401 Interface engine 2 (west) - Block ID 0x402 Interface engine 3 (west) - Block ID 0x403 Interface engine 4 (east) - Block ID 0x500 Interface engine 5 (east) - Block ID 0x501 Interface engine 6 (east) - Block ID 0x502 Interface engine 7 (east) - Block ID 0x503,Sub_block ID number if relevent, now it is a constant value 16'h0

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 0 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 1 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 2 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 3 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 4 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 5 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 6 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 7 1--overflow
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port0
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port1
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port2
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port3
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port4
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port5
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port6
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port7
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 0 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 1 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 2 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 3 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 4 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 5 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 6 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 7 1-link down
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-0
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-1
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-2
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-3
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-4

LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-6
LLR_TX fsm trans to FLUSH of port-7
CBFC_RX lost credit is negtive of port-0
CBFC_RX lost credit is negtive of port-1
CBFC_RX lost credit is negtive of port-2
CBFC_RX lost credit is negtive of port-3

CBFC_RX lost credit is negtive of port-7

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: INTR_STS_MASK_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Interrupt mask. Can be dynamically modified

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 0 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 1 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 2 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 3 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 4 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 5 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 6 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 7 1--overflow
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port0
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port1
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port2
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port3
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port4
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port5
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port6
link status posedge indication.0-no interrupt 1-interrupt each bit per port7
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 0 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 1 1-link down
RX LINK STATUS negedge of channel 2 1-link down

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: INTR_ENABLE_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Interrupt enable. Can be dynamically modified

tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 1 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 2 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 3 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 4 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 5 1--overflow
tx chan buff overflow indication for channel 6 1--overflow

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Single Error Counter: Provides a total single error counter for all memories that have ECC.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description:
ECC Single Error Counter: Provides a total single error counter for all SRAM memories that have ECC. Clear on read. No wrap around.
Soft Errors
Soft errors are a transient random "bit-flip" of memory cells. It is an event that corrupts the value stored in a memory cell without damaging the cell itself.
Soft errors are caused by the following events:
• Alpha particles (helium nucleus of 2 protons +2 neutrons) emissions from chip packaging. This has been largely eliminated by chip manufacturers. • Cosmic rays (high-energy particle originating in outer space). At ground level, cosmic radiation is about 95% neutrons and 5% protons. These particles can cause soft errors directly; and they can also interact with atomic nuclei to produce troublesome short-range heavy ions.
SRAM and DRAM Soft Error Information Description
• All SRAMs and DRAMs have ECC SECDED (Single Error Correction Double Error Detection). SRAMs can also have parity instead of ECC. The ECC is using the Hamming code algorithm with one additional parity bit for double error detection. • All SRAMs and DRAMs with ECC protection have single and double error indication via interrupt. • All SRAMs and DRAMs with ECC protection have double and single error injection.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Double Error Counter: Provides a total double error counter for all memories that have ECC.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Double Error Counter: Provides a total double error counter for all SRAM memories that have ECC. Clear on read. No wrap around.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Address Error Select: Specifies the ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) for register ECC_ADD
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Address Error Select: Specifies the ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) according to register ECC_SINGLE_ERROR / ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR for register ECC_ADDR_ERR

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC ID Write Data: ECC ID of the SRAM When the IDs **40, 41, 42** are incorrectly annotated, they should be mapped to **48, 49, 50**
ECC split number (ECC Split) (ECC may cover sections of an SRAM line).

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_LOW
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Address Error Data Low: Provides the last ECC error address and type.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Address Error Data Low: Provides the ECC error address (line number) for the SRAM ECC ID specified in register ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT (even it does not match field LAST_ERR in registers ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_SERR/ ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_DERR). Register ECC_LATCH_MODE_0 controls if first or last ECC error is stored.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_HIGH
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Address Error Data High: Provides ECC error additional information
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Address Error Data High: Provides ECC error additional information for the SRAM specified in register ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC syndrome (error bit according to the Hamming code).
ECC split number (ECC Split) (ECC may cover sections of an SRAM line).
Single Double Bit. 0 - Single bit error. 1 - Double bit error.
1 - The data in registers ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_LOW and ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_HIGH have been updated from the SRAM selected by register ECC_ADDR_ERROR_SELECT.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: SERR_INJ
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Single Error Injection: Injects a single error (flips one data bit) for specified SRAM ECC ID during a single write.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Single Error Injection: Injects a single error (flips one data bit) for specified SRAM ECCID during a single write. ECCID is defined in ECC_SINGLE_ERR

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID). When the IDs **40, 41, 42** are incorrectly annotated, they should be mapped to **48, 49, 50**
If there are ECC slices in the corresponding SRAM, fill in one of the ECC slices with bit error.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: DERR_INJ
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Double Error Injection: Injects a double error (flips two data bits) for specified SRAM ECC ID during a single write.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: Double Error Injection: Injects a double error (flips two data bits) for specified memory ID during a single write. ID is defined in ECC_DOUBLE_ERR

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID). When the IDs **40, 41, 42** are incorrectly annotated, they should be mapped to **48, 49, 50**
If there are ECC slices in the corresponding SRAM, fill in one of the ECC slices with bit error.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_SERR
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC ID First Last Single Error: Indicates the first and last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID)for the block that had a single error.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC ID First Last Single Error: Indicates the first and last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) for the block that had a single error (e.g. the block had three ECC errors in three different SRAMs). This may be useful understanding multi ECC error events in different memories

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indicates first ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a single error.
Indicates last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a single error.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the first ECC ID SRAM.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the last ECC ID SRAM.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_ID_FIRST_LAST_DERR
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC ID First Last Double Error: Indicates the first and last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) for the block that had a double error.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC ID First Last Double Error: Indicates the first and last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) for the block that had a double error (e.g. the block had three ECC errors in three different SRAMs).

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Indicates first ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a double error.
Indicates last ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID) with a double error.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the first ECC ID SRAM.
1 - More than one ECC error occurred on the last ECC ID SRAM.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Single Error 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Single Error 0: Provides a single error host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity. Bit Value: 0 - SRAM has no error. 1 - SRAM has an error. Bit Description: b0 - Name of ECCID 0 with ECC.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_MASK_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Single Error Mask 0: Provides a single error interrupt mask for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Single Error Mask 0: Provides a single error interrupt mask for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity. The interrupt will be sent to external host if in register ECC_DISABLE_STATUS the status indication is enabled. Bit Value: 0 - Enable ECC single error interrupt for this SRAM. 1 - Disable ECC single error interrupt to external host for this SRAM. Bit Description: b0 - Name of first SRAM with ECC. ...

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Double Error 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Double Error 0: Provides a host interrupt cause for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity. Bit Value: 0 - SRAM has no error. 1 - SRAM has an error. Bit Description: b0 - Name of ECCID 0 with ECC.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_MASK_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Double Error Mask 0: Provides a double error interrupt mask for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Double Error Mask 0: Provides a double error interrupt mask for all SRAMs that have ECC or parity. The interrupt will be sent to external host if in register ECC_DISABLE_STATUS the status indication is enabled. Bit Value: 0 - Enable ECC double error interrupt to external host for this SRAM. 1 - Disable ECC double error interrupt for this SRAM. Bit Description: b0 - Name of first SRAM with ECC. ...

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DISABLE_FIX_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Disable Fix: Disables ECC calculation and fix that corrects single-error in SRAMs that have ECC protection
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Disable Fix: Disables ECC correction that corrects single-error in SRAMs that have ECC protection. Bit Value: 0 - Enable ECC correction (only single errors can be corrected). 1 - Disable ECC correction. Bit Description: b0 - First ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID)

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_DISABLE_STATUS_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Disable Status 0: Disables SRAM ECC status indication.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Disable Status 0: Disables SRAM ECC status indication for memories to host block. An interrupt will be sent to external host if in register ECC_SINGLE_ERROR_MASK or ECC_DOUBLE_ERROR_MASK the interrupt bit is enabled. Bit Value: 0 - Enable ECC status indication to host block. 1 - Disable ECC status indication. Bit Description: b0 - First ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID)

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: ECC_LATCH_MODE_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ECC Latch Mode 0: Indicates if the first or last ECC error will be stored by the SRAM.
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: ECC Latch Mode 0: Indicates if the first or last ECC error (faulty line and syndrome bit) will be stored by the SRAM. This may be useful understanding multi ECC error events. When reading the registers ECC_ADDR_ERROR_DATA_LOW/HIGH the results will be according to register ECC_LATCH_MODE. Bit Value: 1 - First ECC error will be stored by the SRAM. 0 - Last ECC error will be stored by the SRAM. Bit Description: b0 - First ECC ID of the SRAM (ECCID)

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 0 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 0 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 1 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 1 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 2 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 2 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 3 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 3 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 4 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 4 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 5 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 5 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 6 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 6 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 7 overflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 7 underflow
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 0 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 0 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 1 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 1 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 2 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 2 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 3 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 3 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 4 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 4 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 5 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 5 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 6 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 6 empty
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 7 full
cbfc_rx maci cc info fifo 7 empty

0 - Disable 1 - Enable
each bit per lane

0 - Disable 1 - Enable
each bit per lane
If set 1 and macw_egress_ts_valid is 1, set llr_eligible to 0.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_ADAPT_TX_FIFO
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: cfg adapt_tx_fifo_afull_th

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: The configuration value is `port_id`：cnt0=port 0-3，cnt1 = port 4-7 mod 4 bits[2:0] : c nt_0 =0 bits[5:3] : cnt_0 =1 bits[8::6] : cnt_0 =2 bits[11:9] : cnt_0 =3 bits[14:12] : cnt_1 =0 bits[17:15] : cnt_1 =1 bits[20:18] : cnt_1 =2 bits[23:21] : cnt_1 =3

Offset of the first bit of the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits to be driven in the calendar. For example, in order to drive bits 10-19 of first NET_IF_PORT block in the pipe, configure the following: Register LED_CFG_START field CURRENT = 0. Register LED_CFG_END field CURRENT = 19. Register LED_CFG_START field OFFSET = 10.
The first bit in the calendar of 4096 bits that is mapped to the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits. The NET_IF_PORT that is the first in the pipe (master) must start at 0.
The last bit in the calendar of 4096 bits that is mapped to the current NET_IF_PORT block of 128 bits.

The last used bit of the calendar of 4096 bits. This value must be equal to the value of the LED_CFG_END CURRENT field of the last NET_IF_PORT block in the pipe. Valid only for the first NET_IF_PORT in the pipe (master).

LED clock frequency selector: Controls the frequency on pin LED_CLK. Use the same value for all NET_IF_PORT blocks. 0x0 - Core clock frequency / 512. 0x1 - Core clock frequency / 256. 0x2 - Core clock frequency / 128. 0x3 - Core clock frequency / 64. 0x4 - Core clock frequency / 32. 0x5 - Core clock frequency / 16.

Pulse width for dynamic signals (makes the signals visible). Use the same value for all NET_IF_PORT blocks. 0x0 - 2^24 system clock cycles. 0x1 - 2^23 system clock cycles. 0x2 - 2^22 system clock cycles. 0x3 - 2^21 system clock cycles. 0x4 - 2^20 system clock cycles. 0x5 - 2^19 system clock cycles. 0x6 - 2^18 system clock cycles. 0x7 - 2^9 system clock cycles. 0x8 - 2^7 system clock cycles.
Enable: 0 - LED interface is disabled. 1 - LED interface is enabled, and the LED bitmap of 128 bits has valid values.

Master Instance: 0 - This NET_IF_PORT block is not the first (master) in the pipe of NET_IF_PORT blocks. 1 - This NET_IF_PORT block is the first (master) in the pipe of NET_IF_PORT blocks. Only one of the 8 NET_IF_PORT blocks can be master.
User defined bits to be driven via the LED stream. For each bit i, case {LED_OVR_0[i], LED_OVER_1[i]}: 0x3 - On. 0x2 - Blink. 0x1/0x0 - Off.
User defined bits to be driven via the LED stream. For each bit i, case {LED_OVR_0[i], LED_OVER_1[i]}: 0x3 - On. 0x2 - Blink. 0x1/0x0 - Off.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CEMAC_CFG_CTRL_REG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: TX MAC frame control on lane 0:800G,400G,200G,100G,50G,40G

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Frame control for 40G-800G frame. This is a control bus for 50G-800G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 10~100G frame. This is a control bus for 10~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 50~100G frame. This is a control bus for 50~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 10~100G frame. This is a control bus for 10~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 40~400G frame. This is a control bus for 10~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 10~100G frame. This is a control bus for 10~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 50~100G frame. This is a control bus for 50~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

Frame control for 10~100G frame. This is a control bus for 10~100G frame If bit[0] is set to 1, FCS field should be generated and added to transmitted packet, otherwise, user logic should generate CRC for packet. Bit[1] is reserved, should be 0 always If bit[2] is set to 1, source address got from user logic should be replaced by the value in MAC REG, and bit[0] or bit[1] should also be asserted.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: MASK_CFG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: 0 - no mask 1 - mask when it is 1, the tx_adapter can unaffect about link_status . this configration masks link status and, when it is 1, mask link status , or no mask link status. when mac ip is mac loopback , pcs loopback and only tx transmiting data, this reg need to relative bit configration 1, or netif_port output vaild is 0. According to serdes lane configration. 0 - no mask1 - maskwhen it is 1, the rx_adapter can unaffect about link_status . this configration masks link status and, when it is 1, mask link status , or no mask link status. when mac ip is mac loopback , pcs loopback and only tx transmiting data, this reg need to relative bit configration 1, or netif_port output vaild is 0. According to serdes lane configration.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: 0 - no mask 1 - mask when it is 1, the tx_adapter can unaffect about link_status . this configration masks link status and, when it is 1, mask link status , or no mask link status. when mac ip is mac loopback , pcs loopback and only tx transmiting data, this reg need to relative bit configration 1, or netif_port output vaild is 0. According to serdes lane configration.

0 - no mask1 - maskwhen it is 1, the rx_adapter can unaffect about link_status . this configration masks link status and, when it is 1, mask link status , or no mask link status. when mac ip is mac loopback , pcs loopback and only tx transmiting data, this reg need to relative bit configration 1, or netif_port output vaild is 0. According to serdes lane configration.

0 - mask disable 1 - mask enable bit0 - port0 other error bit1 - port0 crc error bit2 - port1 other error bit3 - port1 crc error bit4 - port2 other error bit5 - port2 crc error bit6 - port3 other error bit7 - port3 crc error bit8 - port4 other error bit9 - port4 crc error bit10 - port5 other error bit11 - port5 crc error bit12 - port6 other error bit13 - port6 crc error
bit14 - port7 other error bit15 - port7 crc error

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_RX_OVERTIME
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: overtime threshold for adapt rx

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: rx pkt overtime add_eop (clk : 1.35G)： >= The transmission time of the longest packet + the time ewquired for L1R retry L1R retry time 3us default 3.5us

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_TX_OVERTIME
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: overtime threshold for adapt tx（step value of 5,The actual threshold is the configured value * 5）

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_TX_LINK_DOWN_DROP
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: enable config for drop when link down

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: If bit[0] is set to 1, 800GR8 channel is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 400GR8 channel is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 400GR4 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 400GR4 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 200GR4 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 200GR4 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 200GR4\2 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 200GR2 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 200GR2 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 200GR2 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 100GR2 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 100GR2 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 100GR2 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 100GR2 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[4] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 4 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[5] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 5 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[6] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 6 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[7] is set to 1, 100GR1 channel 7 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 100GR4 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 100GR4 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 50GR1 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 50GR1 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 50GR1 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 50GR1 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 50GR2 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 50GR2 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 50GR2 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 50GR2 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 25GR1 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 25GR1 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 25GR1 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 25GR1 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[0] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 0 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[1] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 1 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[2] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 2 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.
If bit[3] is set to 1, 10GR1 channel 3 is enabled, otherwise, this channel is disabled.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_1588_GLOB_REG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description:
bit 0 Enable IEEE 1588 reporting to TM and Message-DMA bit[1:2] Number of Message-DMA Unit.
bit[3:5] Amount of Message-DMA Credits.
bit 8 Select which TM receives 2-step ACK.
bit 9 Amount of RTC bytes for time tx stamp 0 - 4 Bytes 1 - 5 Bytes
bit 10 Endianness of the 16 Byte header incoming from CTOPs. 0 - Little Endian 1 - Big Endian
bit 11 Endianness of the 2-Step message returned to CTOPs. 0 - Little Endian 1 - Big Endian
bit 12 Correction field overflow enable. 0 - CF calculated 1 - CF overflow code
bit 13 Correction field underflow enable. 0 - CF calculated 1 - CF overflow code
bit 16 RTC enable.
bit 20 Endianness of the partial CF in the header 0 - Little Endian 1 - Big Endian
bit 21 Endianness of the partial CF in the header 0 - Little Endian 1 - Big Endian

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: Enable IEEE 1588 time-stamp in TX 0 - Disable 1 -ENABLE
Enable IEEE 1588 time-stamp in RX 0 - Disable 1 -ENABLE

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CFG_FC_EN
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: flow control enable

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: 1-flow control enable 0-flow control disable Description: Bit 0 port 0 Bit 1 port1 … … Bit 7 port 7
8 port 1-When llr_en = 1, the pause stops sending packets at the pkt boundary of the adapt_tx .

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: TX_DROP_PKT_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: TX channel 0 send disable and lost packet count
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: TX channel 0 send disable and lost packet count

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: RX_DROP_PKT_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: RX channel 0 receive disable and lost packet count
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Full_Description: channel 0 ~ channel7

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: TX_PKT_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: maci to ada_input pkt cntr pkt cntr：pkt cnt、double_sop、double_eop

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: RX_PKT_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: ada_output to maci pkt cntr：whole pkt 、double_sop、double_eop

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: 1-seq_num msb order is tx_seq[19:4]; 0-seq_num msb order is {tx_seq[11:4], tx_seq[19:12]}.
O-code of CTLOS, also used for CBFC_TX, CBFC_RX
When set, FSM will trans to flush from replay state if pcs_lost_timer or data_age_timer expired
Only used for port-0, waiting clks for splicing packet, 0 means not wait
The data read from debug cnt register belongs to the choosen port
Configures the mode of operation of the LLR. Once this is set, LLR_INIT may be sent. Values: 0-OFF, 1-ON
Configures the behavior of the TX LLR when the TX state machine is in the INIT state. Values: 0-BEST_EFFORT, 1-BLOCK, 2-DISCARD, 3-RESERVED
Configures the behavior of the TX LLR when the TX state machine is in the FLUSH state. Values: 0-BEST_EFFORT, 1-BLOCK, 2-DISCARD, 3-RESERVED

Configures the behavior in the event of an LLR replay failure. Values: 0-FALSE, 1-TRUE When the value is TRUE, then the LLR will attempt to re-initialize. When the value is FALSE, then the LLR will wait for management intervention.
LLR TX timers resolution, used for replay_timer, pcs_lost_status_timer and data_age_timer. Values: 0-1clk, 1-2clk, … , F-16clk.
LLR TX timers pause when receive pause frame.
A configuration of init data in INIT ctlos, it will be used when init_data_en is set.

A configuration indicating the target number of bytes between transmission of INIT ctlos, resolution is 64bytes. It should be four times larger than the target_spacing (which resolution is 1byte). Max value is 1024
The delay (clks) of of sending INIT-CtlOS after fsm trans to INIT. Used to prevent INIT-CtlOS insert into replay_eligible frames.
A configuration to set the value of replay_timer at which replay_timer_expired is set and a replay is initiated. Values: 0 to 65,535

A configuration to set the maximum number of times a replay is performed before the LLR mechanism gives up and enters the FLUSH state. A value of all-ones (0xFF) is used to indicate that there is no maximum. Values: 0 to 255

The value at which the pcs_lost_status_timer is considered to have expired. Used to progress the LLR transmit state machine into the FLUSH state when the PCS_status has been FALSE for too long (even if there are no unacknowledged frames).

The value at which the data_age_timer is considered to have expired. data_age_timer used to time how long data has been resident in the replay buffer without any forward progress being made on acknowledging the data. Expiry of the data_age_timer will result in the data held in the replay buffer being discarded.
Configures the mode of operation of the LLR. Once this is set, an LLR_INIT may be received. Values: 0-OFF, 1-ON
A configuration indicating the target number of bytes between transmission of successive LLR_ACK / LLR_NACK CtlOS. Values 400 to 16384 with suggested value 2048.
A count of the number of LLR_INIT Control Ordered Sets transmitted.

A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames discarded by the LLR TX when the TX state machine is in the INIT state and the llr_init_behavior is set to DISCARD, or when the TX state machine is in the FLUSH state and the llr_flush_behavior is set to DISCARD.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames transmitted with a good FCS.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames transmitted with a poisoned FCS.
A count of the number of times that the transmitter exited the REPLAY state (i.e., completed a replay operation).
A count of the number of LLR_INIT_ECHO Control Ordered Sets received.
A count of the number of LLR_ACK Control Ordered Sets received.
A count of the number of LLR_NACK Control Ordered Sets received.
A count of LLR_ACK/LLR_NACK sequence number errors. The counter is incremented each time an LLR_ACK or LLR_NACK is received for which ack_nack_seq_ok is FALSE.
A count of the number of LLR-ineligible frames.
A count of the number of LLR_TX FSM trans to INIT.
A count of the number of LLR_TX FSM trans to FLUSH.
A count of the number of times that the LLR_TX FSM trans to REPLAY state due to replay_timer_expired.

[2:0] - fsm state: 3'd0-off, 3'd1-init, 3'd2-advance, 3'd3-replay, 3'd4-flush [15:3] - outstanding_seq [27:16] - buffer_cnt [39:28] - eop_mem_cnt: equal to outstanding_seq [42:40] - flush reason: {ct_max, data_age, pcs_lost} [63:43] - reserved
A count of the number of LLR_INIT_ECHO Control Ordered Sets transmitted.
A count of the number of LLR_ACK Control Ordered Sets transmitted.
A count of the number of LLR_NACK Control Ordered Sets transmitted.
A count of the number of LLR_INIT Control Ordered Sets received.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a good FCS.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a poisoned FCS.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a bad FCS.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a good FCS that had the expected sequence number.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a poisoned FCS that had the expected sequence number.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received with a bad FCS that had the expected sequence number.
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received that had a sequence number that indicated a missing LLR-eligible frame in the sequence (irrespective of FCS status).
A count of the number of LLR-eligible frames received that had a duplicate sequence number (irrespective of FCS status).
A count of the number of times that the receiver has detected the start of a replay.
A count of the number of LLR-ineligible frames.

Configuration of port-0 credit_size: [1:0]: 0-div 4; 1-div 5; 2-div 6; 3-div 7. [4:2]: 0-div 8; 1-div 16; 2-div 32; 3-div 64; 4-div 128; 5-div 256; 6-div 512. for example: [4:2]=5, [1:0]=0, CREDIT_SIZE=256*4=1024; [4:2]=5, [1:0]=3, CREDIT_SIZE=256*7=1792. default value is 128B.
Configuration of port-0 pkt_ovhd. Values: -16 to 127
A configuration bitmap for all VCs of a port: 1-lossless, 0-best_effort

Configuration of port-0 credit_size: [1:0]: 0-div 4; 1-div 5; 2-div 6; 3-div 7. [4:2]: 0-div 8; 1-div 16; 2-div 32; 3-div 64; 4-div 128; 5-div 256; 6-div 512. for example: [4:2]=5, [1:0]=0, CREDIT_SIZE=256*4=1024; [4:2]=5, [1:0]=3, CREDIT_SIZE=256*7=1792. default value is 128B.
Configuration of port-0 pkt_ovhd. Values: -16 to 127
Configurable minimum time (bytes) between CF_Update CtlOS (applied to updated R_VC_CF[x] values). Values: 800B to 16kB.
configurable average maximum time (bytes) between CF_Update CtlOS (for all R_VC_CF[x] values). Values: 16kB to 1MB.
1-check MACDA (not check SA!) when receive CC_Update; 0-not check.
Enable for the shaping of CF return to TX: 1-enable shaping, 0-disable. Shaping RTC is configured in CBFC_RX_CFG_1.
A configuration bitmap for all VCs of a port: 1-lossless, 0-best_effort

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_CC_UPDATE_0
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Configurations for CC_Update message packet fields.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: MAC Destination Address. Set to 01-80-C2-00-00-01 or the 48-bit individual address of the destination station. Should be configured in big-endian order.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_CC_UPDATE_1
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Configurations for CC_Update message packet fields.

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: MAC Source Address. Set to the 48-bit individual address of the source station.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_CC_UPDATE_2
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: Configurations for CC_Update message packet fields.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_DEBUG_CFG
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: CBFC debug port and VC configurations, both for TX and RX

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description: debug port for CBFC TX and RX

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_DEBUG_TX_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: CBFC TX counters of the {port, vc} configured by CBFC_DEBUG_CFG

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_DEBUG_RX_CNT
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: CBFC RX counters of the {port, vc} configured by CBFC_DEBUG_CFG

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_TX_ERR_INFO
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: CBFC TX error info for all ports

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description:
[7:0] - port 0, [15:8] - port 1, ... [63:56] - port 7.
8bits state per port: bit[0] - tm_vc_cc_diff neg, bit[1] - return credit of cf_update neg, bit[2] - return credit to TM neg, bit[7:3] - reserved.

registerdb:Register.registerdb:Name: CBFC_RX_ERR_INFO
registerdb:Register.registerdb:Short_Description: CBFC RX error info for all ports

registerdb:Field.registerdb:Description:
[7:0] - port 0, [15:8] - port 1, ... [63:56] - port 7.
8bits state per port: bit[0] - lost credit neg, bit[1] - b-type pkt and cc_update is llr-ineligible, bit[7:2] - reserved.
10 bits mantissa of the maximum number of credits that can be accumulated. value 0x000 is reserved for LIMIT==WEIGHT (in credits unit)
Limit exponent 0x0 - x1 0x1 - x4 0x2 - x16 0x3 - x64
